name: simple-dev
title: Simple Dev
job_to_be_done: Apply simple development constraints when the user explicitly asks for minimal or straightforward work, fewer abstractions, files, or jumps, flatter control flow, lightweight test-first changes, or N+1 avoidance. Use across planning, implementation, refactoring, and code review. Follow existing project conventions; do not replace dedicated TDD, architecture design, performance diagnosis, security review, or migration workflows.

trigger_surface.description: Apply simple development constraints when the user explicitly asks for minimal or straightforward work, fewer abstractions, files, or jumps, flatter control flow, lightweight test-first changes, or N+1 avoidance. Use across planning, implementation, refactoring, and code review. Follow existing project conventions; do not replace dedicated TDD, architecture design, performance diagnosis, security review, or migration workflows.
should_trigger: Use $simple-dev to make the smallest correct change in Codex.
/simple-dev refactor this module with fewer layers while preserving behavior.
/simple-dev review this workspace change for unnecessary abstractions and N+1 risks.
Load the simple-dev skill and plan the minimal implementation.
Fix this bug with the smallest change and keep the implementation straightforward.
Keep this implementation minimal and straightforward, with as few abstractions and files as practical.
Plan the smallest change that satisfies this requirement; do not design future architecture.
Refactor this service into fewer layers while preserving behavior.
Review this diff for unnecessary abstraction and N+1 queries.
请用最小改动修复这个问题，保持实现简单。
检查这段查询有没有 N+1，不要引入多余层级。
Let the architecture workflow lead this architecture redesign, but keep the proposal minimal with fewer layers.
should_not_trigger: 只运行测试，不要修改代码。
edge_cases: Use strict TDD and follow the full red green refactor loop.
Design the architecture for a new distributed system.
Run a performance diagnosis and identify the CPU bottleneck.
对这个认证模块做安全审计。

steps: Confirm the request explicitly asks for minimal, straightforward, low-abstraction, or N+1-aware development.
Classify the activity as planning, implementation, refactoring, or read-only review.
Inspect project conventions, public interfaces, acceptance criteria, and the relevant verification baseline.
Choose and execute the smallest correct approach without unrelated cleanup or speculative architecture.
Run relevant tests and directly affected checks, then return every field in the output contract.
decision_points: Correctness, safety, explicit requirements, project conventions, and acceptance criteria take priority over simplicity preferences.
Add an abstraction or file only for project conventions, a real repeated responsibility, a clear complexity reduction, or a necessary test boundary.
Prefer batch database access, but preserve per-item transactions, locks, streaming, memory limits, rate limits, and established project semantics when required.
Let dedicated TDD, architecture, performance, security, or migration workflows lead their specialist tasks.
Stop scope expansion and request direction when a conflict cannot be resolved safely from repository evidence.
failure_modes: The skill activates for generic development requests without an explicit simplicity signal.
A mechanically smaller design violates correctness, safety, project conventions, or a public interface.
Unrelated cleanup or speculative abstractions expand the requested change.
A blanket ban on per-item database operations changes transaction or locking semantics.
The final response invents test, runtime, database, or native-client evidence that was not observed.
